page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

The x86_64 architecture of the Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix mathematics. Cryptographic operations are supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instructions. The processor features a large 480 MB L3 cache alongside 2 MB of L2 cache per core, which supports memory-intensive operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text processing, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench text processing and small-model LLM text generation at 350.71 tokens per second. It also achieves strong top-25% results in single-threaded gzip compression, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations. However, memory bandwidth for uncached large-block writes is moderate at 77.15 GB/sec, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is weak, averaging 2.75 tokens per second.

The m8i-flex.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads. Its qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the flex-class resource allocation, making it a viable option for workloads that require high single-core and multi-core CPU performance without the premium of dedicated local NVMe storage. Best-fit workloads include static web serving, text processing, database operations, and small-to-medium scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits strong performance in text processing, single-threaded compression, and small-model LLM text generation, though it shows limitations in large-model LLM text generation. With a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads. It is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, database operations, and medium-scale enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It offers strong text processing and compression performance but lacks local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.8xlarge is an Intel Xeon-based general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 128 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 3.9 GHz clock speed and 480 MB L3 cache
Hardware acceleration for matrix math (AMX) and cryptography (AES, SHA_NI)
Strong performance in text processing, compression, and small-model LLM inference
No local storage or dedicated GPU, relying on network-attached storage
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications
